mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a win rate above 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions signal supply chain bottlenecks and profit margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not turn negative following INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, aggressive monetary tightening restricts market liquidity and forces equity valuation multiples to contract over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a win rate above 58% in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly expansions in real gross domestic product reflect broad-based economic growth that translates into higher corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicates healthy capacity utilization and improving corporate output, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns do not turn positive when INDPRO exceeds 102 in recovery regimes, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.
